episodes.title: Florida Stacks Two Defenders in a Day: Cahron Wheeler & Kamarion Johnson Commit for 2027
episodes.description: Florida just landed back-to-back commitments on the defensive side of the ball, and both stories are worth your time. Four-star edge Cahron Wheeler picks the Gators on his official visit, then athlete Kamarion Johnson follows right after over Florida State and Cincinnati. We dig into the recruitments, the evaluations, and what Jon Sumrall's staff is putting together for 2027. episodes.title: Gator Tales #60: Duke Werner - Executive Associate Athletics Director / Director of Sport
episodes.description: In the latest episode of Gator Tales, Presented by UF Health, Voice of the Gators Sean Kelley sits down for an exclusive conversation with Executive Associate Athletics Director/Director of Sport Dave Werner. During Episode 60, Kelley and Werner look back on the 2025-26 athletic year and discuss his new role with the Gators. They also dive into recent UF coaching changes along with a slew of other topics in a candid and informative visit for Gator Nation.

episodes.title: Gator Tales #59: March Madness Preview
episodes.description: his week on Gator Tales, Presented by UF Health, Voice of the Gators Sean Kelley hosts a Men's NCAA Tournament roundtable discussion with current radio analysts and former Gators guards Lee Humphrey and Brian Hogan. episodes.description: This week on Gator Tales, Presented by UF Health, Voice of the Gators Sean Kelley sits down with a pair of Florida stars on the diamond in Aidan King and Ava Brown as the spring sports schedule hits full force. Kelley catches up with King on the heels of his first weekend start of the 2026 season, having chucked six shutout innings to earn the win against UAB on Feb. 14. The two discuss King's second act in Orange & Blue after a historic rookie campaign one year ago in which the Jacksonville, Fl

episodes.title: Gator Tales #57: Buster Faulkner
episodes.description: This week on Gator Tales, Presented by UF Health, Voice of the Gators Sean Kelley provides the first exclusive interview with new UF Football Offensive Coordinator Buster Faulkner. Hired by Head Football Coach Jon Sumrall on Dec. 11, Faulkner arrived in Gator Country after three successful seasons as the offensive coordinator at Georgia Tech (2023-25). He comes equipped with 14 years of experience as an offensive coordinator alone, also coaching the tight ends at Georgia Tech after three seasons

episodes.title: Gator Tales #56: Kayla DiCello
episodes.description: This week on Gator Tales, Presented by UF Health, Voice of the Gators Sean Kelley sits down with junior All-American Kayla DiCello as she prepares for a long-awaited return to competition in Orange and Blue. The 2023 Southeastern Conference Freshman of the Year stepped away from collegiate competition to chase a dream—earning a spot on the U.S. Olympic Team—but her journey was interrupted by an Achilles injury sustained at the U.S. Olympic Trials. After a season of recovery and reflection, DiCel

episodes.title: Gator Tales #55: Jon Sumrall & Nick Zimmerman
episodes.description: Episode 55 of Gator Tales with Sean Kelley, Presented by UF Health brings a pair of newly-minted UF head coaches into the conversation in football's Jon Sumrall and soccer's Nick Zimmerman. The episode opens with a fireside chat featuring Sumrall alongside legendary Gators football coaches Steve Spurrier and Urban Meyer. The trio dives into Sumrall's background and vision for the UF football program during an exciting transition period in Gainesville. Thereafter, Kelley passes the ball to Ryan U

episodes.title: Gator Tales #54: Urban Meyer and Kelly Rae Finley
episodes.description: Episode 54 of Gator Tales with Sean Kelley, Presented by UF Health is headlined by interviews with former Gators national champion football coach Urban Meyer and current Florida head women's basketball coach Kelly Rae Finley. The podcast begins with Meyer, who discusses his upcoming induction into the College Football Hall of Fame as a member of the 2025 class in December. Kelley and Meyer also preview the former's honorary return to The Swamp during the Tennessee game on Nov. 22, culminating wi

episodes.title: Gator Tales with Sean Kelley: Men's Hoops Preview with Todd Golden
episodes.description: Gator Tales with Sean Kelley, Presented by UF Health hits the hardwood this week as Episode 53 features head men's basketball coach Todd Golden ahead of Florida's Nov. 3 season opener vs. Arizona. With the 2026 campaign set to tip off in Las Vegas in less than two weeks, Golden and Kelley discuss the busy offseason for the reigning National Champions. Golden also previews the upcoming season with high expectations in store once again for the AP's Preseason No. 3 team. In cutting down the nets la

episodes.title: Gator Tales #52: The Gatorade Story with Dr. Chip Hinton & Dr. Christian Jobin
episodes.description: Gator Tales with Sean Kelley, Presented by UF Health is back in action this week to celebrate the 60th anniversary of Gatorade. Episode 52 of Gator Tales dives into the story of Gatorade via a pair of special guests in Dr. Chip Hinton and Dr. Christian Jobin. These conversations kick off a weekend of celebration for 60 years of Gatorade, culminating with recognition during Saturday's home football game against Texas. Kelley begins Episode 52 with Dr. Hinton, who was one of the "Baby Gators" and

episodes.title: Gator Tales #51: George Gumbs Jr. & Samantha Bohon
episodes.description: Episode 51 of Gator Tales touches base with the football and women's soccer programs as they hit the thick of their regular-season schedules. Kelley connects with Gators edge rusher George Gumbs Jr., who enters Week 4 as the team leader in tackles for loss in his second year in Orange & Blue. Head soccer coach Samantha Bohon also joins the show as Florida progresses through SEC play with a pair of upcoming top-25 matchups against No. 24 Oklahoma and No. 15 South Carolina.

episodes.title: Gator Tales #50: J. Michael Sturdivant, Alex Condon
episodes.description: Episode 50 of Gator Tales features a conversation with UF Men's Basketball National Champion Alex Condon, as well as Gators Football transfer wide receiver J. Michael Sturdivant. With the 2025 football season opener just around the corner on Sept. 30 vs. LIU (7 p.m.), Sturdivant is in line for his first-career game in The Swamp after spending the four previous campaigns at UCLA and Cal.
